name: Business objects and data modeling
summary: Understand how entities, fields, relationships and lifecycle states are structured so workflows, forms, searches and reporting remain stable and scalable.
interviewAngles: How would you design a new object?
How do relationships affect reporting and workflow logic?
How do you prevent poor field design from becoming long-term technical debt?
reviewChecklist: Map primary records, supporting records and relationship direction.
Define required fields, state logic and ownership clearly.
Review reporting and permission impacts before deployment.

name: Forms, layouts and workspace usability
summary: Designing the right experience for different user types while preserving clarity, governance and maintainability.
interviewAngles: How do you simplify forms for self-service users?
How do you enforce required data without harming usability?
How do you organize layouts for technician efficiency?
reviewChecklist: Apply visibility and edit logic intentionally.
Test on real workflows, not only on form design screens.

name: Saved searches and operational filtering
summary: Saved searches often drive technician views, dashboards, automation inputs and scheduled communication patterns.
interviewAngles: How do you avoid overly broad search definitions?
How do you reuse saved searches operationally?
How do you validate search accuracy before scheduling actions?
reviewChecklist: Use precise conditions and ownership filters.
Document where each saved search is reused.

name: Workflow automation strategy
summary: A recurring interview theme across Ivanti products: how to turn repetitive work into safe, supportable automation.
interviewAngles: How do you choose automation boundaries?
How do you test conditions and branches?
How do you prevent duplicate or conflicting actions?
reviewChecklist: Define triggers, conditions and rollback approach.
Separate business intent from implementation detail.
Plan logging and failure handling from the start.

name: Quick actions and operator efficiency
summary: Low-friction actions that help operators move faster while preserving consistency across common tasks.
interviewAngles: When is a quick action better than a bigger workflow?
What guardrails do you add to operator-triggered actions?
How do you make actions discoverable but safe?
reviewChecklist: Test output quality and user comprehension.

name: Email templates and notification design
summary: Notifications are high-impact and high-risk: useful when precise, noisy when overused.
interviewAngles: How do you reduce alert fatigue?
How do you personalize messages by role or audience?
How do you test data merge fields and delivery timing?

name: Dashboards, reporting and metrics
summary: Interviewers often expect you to explain which metrics matter operationally and how reporting supports decision-making.
interviewAngles: Which metrics would you show leadership versus operations?
How do you prevent dashboards from becoming vanity views?
How do you validate data quality behind a KPI?
reviewChecklist: Design separate views for executives and operators.

name: Roles, permissions and access governance
summary: Access design affects data visibility, operational safety and separation of duties across all enterprise tools.
interviewAngles: How do you structure roles without creating access sprawl?
How do you troubleshoot unexpected record visibility?
How do you document ownership for permission changes?
reviewChecklist: Map roles to job function, not individuals.
Test both visibility and edit permissions.

name: REST APIs and integration patterns
summary: Used for inbound and outbound automation, enrichment, synchronization and orchestration with adjacent platforms.
interviewAngles: How do you secure API integrations?
How do you handle pagination, retries and partial failures?
How do you make field mappings supportable?
reviewChecklist: Define authentication, error handling and ownership.
Log request and response outcomes appropriately.

name: Endpoint discovery and inventory health
summary: Accurate inventory underpins deployment, patching, policy enforcement and reporting in endpoint-focused interviews.
interviewAngles: Why does inventory quality matter operationally?
How do you handle stale or incomplete endpoint records?
How do you validate targeting before deployment?
reviewChecklist: Define discovery frequency and reconciliation logic.

name: Software distribution strategy
summary: Packaging, targeting, maintenance windows and rollback thinking are common for Endpoint Manager interviews.
interviewAngles: How do you stage a large rollout safely?
How do you identify deployment blockers early?
How do you communicate failures and next actions?
reviewChecklist: Use clear targeting and success criteria.

name: Patch management and remediation windows
summary: Combines operational urgency, risk-based prioritization, maintenance timing and validation discipline.
interviewAngles: How do you prioritize critical patches?
How do you handle exceptions?
How do you report patch posture accurately?

name: Self-healing and bot governance
summary: Automated remediation can be powerful, but interviews often focus on how you decide what should self-heal and what should still require human review.
interviewAngles: What issues are safe to remediate automatically?
How do you avoid harmful bot actions?
How do you measure self-heal success?
reviewChecklist: Track action outcomes and user impact.

name: Device enrollment models
summary: Enrollment design shapes ownership, security, user experience and downstream support operations.
interviewAngles: How do enrollment methods differ for BYOD and corporate devices?
What are common enrollment failure points?
How do you plan for user communication and support?

name: Compliance and policy enforcement
summary: Policy design should align risk, device posture and user experience without causing unnecessary operational friction.
interviewAngles: How do you choose enforcement actions?
How do you balance security with usability?
How do you troubleshoot inconsistent policy application?
reviewChecklist: Separate critical controls from informational checks.
Use staged testing before broad enforcement.

name: App distribution and mobile release control
summary: Managing internal and public apps across device groups while keeping deployment reliable and supportable.
interviewAngles: How do you stage app releases?
What do you validate before broad deployment?
How do you handle version drift or failed installs?
reviewChecklist: Monitor install success and user feedback.

name: Legacy workflow modernization with Velocity
summary: Focuses on improving operational workflows without forcing full back-end replacement projects.
interviewAngles: Why modernize a host app instead of rebuilding it?
How do you improve mobile usability for existing processes?
How do you measure operational gain?
reviewChecklist: Map current user pain points first.
Design the improved task flow around real operators.
Validate on devices and peripherals used in production.

name: Integration error handling and supportability
summary: Stable integrations require observability, reprocessing options, clear ownership and sane retry logic.
interviewAngles: How do you design retries safely?
How do you alert on failed messages?
How do you prevent duplicate records?

name: Release readiness and change promotion
summary: Enterprise interviews often probe how you move from build to test to production with confidence and minimal disruption.
interviewAngles: What does a strong deployment checklist include?
How do you validate in lower environments?
How do you plan rollback?
reviewChecklist: Use test cases that mirror production usage.

name: Structured troubleshooting discipline
summary: Strong candidates explain how they isolate symptoms, validate hypotheses, use logs and protect service continuity under pressure.
interviewAngles: How do you troubleshoot without making the issue worse?
What evidence do you gather first?
How do you communicate during high-impact incidents?
reviewChecklist: Document fix, validation and preventive follow-up.
